# 111 Network

> Free, Secure Communication for Everyone. A global mesh messaging network that runs on people, not servers — works with phones, radios, satellites, anything that can carry a message. No company. No government.

## About

111 Network is a peer-to-peer mesh messaging protocol that prioritizes resilience over reach. The network is composed of eight subsystems:

- 01 The Protocol — message envelope, routing, signatures
- 02 The Mesh — peer-to-peer transport topology
- 03 Distributed Ledger — tamper-evident message log
- 04 Private Messages — end-to-end encrypted DMs
- 05 Offline Mode — store-and-forward when disconnected
- 06 Multi-Transport — radio, phone, email, satellite
- 07 Identity & Privacy — pseudonymous, key-based identity
- 08 Network Bridges — gateways to other protocols

Current release: v0.0.5 Experimental.

## Resources

- [Homepage](https://111.network/)

## Brand

- Tagline: Free, Secure Communication for Everyone
- Principle: Built by people, not providers
- Aesthetic: monochrome, pixel typography, terminal voice
